independence: medium (independent retrieved source + multi-judge entailment; the support verdict is model-based, flagged)
consensus_rule: reject only on a STRICT MAJORITY 'contradicts' (avoids a single over-strict judge over-blocking a correct claim)

live_demo.claim: Water boils at 90 C at sea level
Water boils at 100 C at sea level

finding: The semantic frontier — a REAL, correctly-attributed source whose FINDING is misstated — is irreducibly a model judgment, so this verifier is held to the no-overclaim posture: an INDEPENDENT retrieved source (Wikipedia, independent of the answer model) judged by a MULTI-JUDGE panel (DeepSeek + Claude), acting only on STRICT-MAJORITY consensus, fail-OPEN on insufficiency. Live demo 4/4 correct: misstated facts -> contradicts (caught), correct facts -> supports/insufficient (passed, no false positive). Independence is MEDIUM and labelled as such — it complements the high-independence layers, it does not replace them. Honest bound: coverage is retrieval-bounded (resolving the right source/title is the bottleneck, as throughout this session) and the verdict is model-based; a full-pack run is gated by source-title resolution.
